site.name: Know Your Republic

home.heading: Know Your Republic
home.intro: A reference directory of the people who govern India, the laws in force, and the rights every citizen holds. Every explained fact links to its official source.

me.title: Overview

constitution.description: The Constitution of India is the country's supreme law. Adopted by the Constituent Assembly on 26 November 1949 and in force since 26 January 1950, it establishes the structure of the Union and state governments, divides powers between them, and sets out the fundamental rights and duties of every citizen. Every other law, and every act of government, must conform to it. With the amendments made since 1950, it is the longest written constitution of any sovereign nation.
constitution.contentsIntro: The Constitution is organised into a Preamble, 22 Parts (some since repealed or renumbered), and 12 Schedules, comprising nearly 470 Articles as amended. Its major divisions:
constitution.downloadsIntro: The official text, published by the Legislative Department, Ministry of Law and Justice, free to view or download.
constitution.pendingLanguages: Editions in the remaining scheduled languages will be added here as official texts are verified.
